short_def: Your chance to win the pot if all cards fall, expressed as a percentage.
reaction_text: Your chance to win the pot if all cards fall, expressed as a percentage.

short_def: Expected Value, the long-run average result of repeating the same decision.
reaction_text: Expected Value, the long-run average result of repeating the same decision.

short_def: The cost of a call compared to the current pot size, shown as a ratio or percentage.
reaction_text: The cost of a call compared to the current pot size, shown as a ratio or percentage.

short_def: Extra future money you expect to win when your hand improves, making a marginal call profitable.
reaction_text: Extra future money you expect to win when your hand improves, making a marginal call profitable.

short_def: How much of your raw equity you actually turn into chips by the river.
reaction_text: How much of your raw equity you actually turn into chips by the river.

short_def: When one player’s range connects better to the board than the opponent’s range.
reaction_text: When one player’s range connects better to the board than the opponent’s range.

short_def: How often you choose a particular line across similar spots to stay balanced.
reaction_text: How often you choose a particular line across similar spots to stay balanced.

short_def: The natural swings up and down that happen even with perfect decisions.
reaction_text: The natural swings up and down that happen even with perfect decisions.

short_def: Stack-to-Pot Ratio, the number of stack big blinds compared to the pot size.
reaction_text: Stack-to-Pot Ratio, the number of stack big blinds compared to the pot size.

short_def: Cards in your hand that reduce the opponent’s strong combinations and improve your bluff chances.
reaction_text: Cards in your hand that reduce the opponent’s strong combinations and improve your bluff chances.

short_def: Emotion-driven play after frustration that harms decision quality.
reaction_text: Emotion-driven play after frustration that harms decision quality.

short_def: A repetitive mistake or pattern that opponents can exploit.
reaction_text: A repetitive mistake or pattern that opponents can exploit.

short_def: A deliberate deviation from baseline play to profit from an opponent’s leak.
reaction_text: A deliberate deviation from baseline play to profit from an opponent’s leak.

short_def: A range made mostly of medium-strength hands that target calls, not folds.
reaction_text: A range made mostly of medium-strength hands that target calls, not folds.

short_def: A range mixing very strong hands and bluffs to pressure opponents.
reaction_text: A range mixing very strong hands and bluffs to pressure opponents.

short_def: A turn bet made after the opponent checks back the flop to steal initiative or shape the hand.
reaction_text: A turn bet made after the opponent checks back the flop to steal initiative or shape the hand.

short_def: When opponents fold too often, allowing you to pressure them profitably.
reaction_text: When opponents fold too often, allowing you to pressure them profitably.

short_def: A small value bet sized to be called by slightly worse hands.
reaction_text: A small value bet sized to be called by slightly worse hands.

short_def: Extra equity you need because busting (or losing chips) costs more than winning them.
reaction_text: Extra equity you need because busting (or losing chips) costs more than winning them.

short_def: Independent Chip Model, the tournament math that values chips by payouts instead of cash.
reaction_text: Independent Chip Model, the tournament math that values chips by payouts instead of cash.

short_def: The money set aside to absorb variance so you can keep playing under pressure.
reaction_text: The money set aside to absorb variance so you can keep playing under pressure.

short_def: The chance your opponent folds to your bet or raise before showdown.
reaction_text: The chance your opponent folds to your bet or raise before showdown.
